Then pass this item to the [doc://com.apple.fileprovider/documentation/FileProvider/NSFileProviderManager/enumeratorForPendingItems()] method on a [doc://com.apple.fileprovider/documentation/FileProvider/NSFileProviderManager] instance for your domain. # pendingItemsDidChange(completionHandler:)

**Instance Method**

Tells the file provider extension that the set of pending items has changed.

## Declaration

```swift
optional func pendingItemsDidChange(completionHandler: @escaping @Sendable () -> Void)
```

```swift
optional func pendingItemsDidChange() async
```

## Parameters

- **completionHandler**: A block that you call after you finish processing the changes.

## Discussion



The system calls this method whenever the set of pending items changes. It updates the pending set regularly, but only when there are meaningful changes, such as:

- New items are now pending.
- The system has successfully synced one or more pending items.
- The domain version changed when the pending item set wasn’t empty.

To enumerate the pending set, create an object that adopts the [doc://com.apple.fileprovider/documentation/FileProvider/NSFileProviderEnumerationObserver] and [doc://com.apple.fileprovider/documentation/FileProvider/NSFileProviderChangeObserver] protocols. Then pass this item to the [doc://com.apple.fileprovider/documentation/FileProvider/NSFileProviderManager/enumeratorForPendingItems()] method on a [doc://com.apple.fileprovider/documentation/FileProvider/NSFileProviderManager] instance for your domain. The system then calls your observer object’s methods when the pending set changes.
title: pendingItemsDidChange(completionHandler:)
